product:
Here is the Python code to select the top 3 matching products based on your query "pendrive" and return them as a JSON list:

```python
import json
from operator import itemgetter

products = [{'id': 1, 'title': 'Fjallraven - Foldsack No. 1 Backpack, Fits 15 Laptops', 'price': 109.95, 'description': 'Your perfect pack for everyday use and walks in the forest. Stash your laptop (up to 15 inches) in the padded sleeve, your everyday', 'category': "men's clothing", 'image': 'https://fakestoreapi.com/img/81fPKd-2AYL._AC_SL1500_t.png', 'rating': {'rate': 3.9, 'count': 120}}, 
            ...]  # rest of the products list

matching_products = [product for product in products if "pendrive" in product['title'].lower()]

if len(matching_products) > 0:
    matching_products.sort(key=itemgetter('price'))  # sort by price
    top_3_matching_products = matching_products[:3]  # select top 3
    result = [{"id": product['id'], "title": product['title'], "price": product['price']} for product in top_3_matching_products]
    print(json.dumps(result))
else:
    print("No matching products found.")
```

This code first creates a list of products. Then it filters the products to only include those whose title contains the query "pendrive" (case-insensitive). If any matches are found, it sorts them by price and selects the top 3. Finally, it converts these products into a JSON list with the desired attributes ("id", "title", and "price") and prints it.

Please note that you'll need to modify this code to fit your specific use case and product data structure.
